findings.finding: Tests and the architecture validator read docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json (and optionally docs/closeout-packs/cp00-136/manifest.json) to assert plannedPack.included_units.length === 150 and exact unit order via validateAuditComplianceCp136Coverage. Neither file is in this diff, so plan/manifest alignment cannot be confirmed here. Non-blocking because the gate is self-protecting: if the plan lacks CP00-136 or its 150 units are missing/misordered, assert.ok(plannedPack) and the coverage order check fail, preventing production_ready. Adjudicator should confirm the plan/manifest include CP00-136 (150 units, RP03.P01.M08.S06..RP03.P02.M07.S06) before relying on a green gate.

createAuditComplianceCp136HermesEvidencePacket stamps every supplied command with status:"passed" unconditionally — a static evidence declaration, not a verified execution result. This honors the no-Hermes-runtime-write boundary (it executes nothing), but downstream consumers should not treat the packet as proof that the commands actually ran/passed; verification remains the responsibility of the test runner and validator.

parsed_review.recommendation: Close CP00-136 after adjudicating non-blocking P3 findings; no P0/P1/P2 findings block production_ready.
parsed_review.rationale: Read-only Risk C service-interface readiness pack CP00-136. The new catalog is pure, frozen, synthetic-only descriptor data: it generates exactly 150 unique covered units (first RP03.P01.M08.S06, last RP03.P02.M07.S06), and deliverable-type distribution (impl 70, ui 23, contract 8, security_audit 16, claude_review 5, failure_recovery 10, test 18) is internally consistent with both the tests and the validator. No code path appends/mutates/deletes/queries/exports/renders audit events, writes product state, executes tenant/matter/permission/idempotency/lock/persistence/rollback/retry logic, writes Hermes runtime, or executes Claude; all no-write attestation flags and the contract block are false/consistent. Contract bumped v0.2->v0.3 with matching pack/next-subphase/next-pack updates; HRX not split, LDIP not implemented; H03/C03 gates declared. Only minor non-blocking notes (external plan/manifest dependency enforced by the gate; static Hermes "passed" labeling).
